description: Strict contract for docx mode outputs. The result must be a complete, sectioned, markdown-friendly intermediate representation suitable for later document generation, not an outline, notes dump, or presentation artifact.

requirements: The document must contain exactly one top-level title.
The title must clearly identify the document subject.
The title must not be empty, generic, or placeholder text.
The body must be organized into explicit sections.
Each section must include a heading and substantive content.
Sections may include subsections when needed for clarity.
Section ordering must be intentional and support a coherent reading flow.
Content must be long-form prose or structured explanatory text, not bullets alone unless the topic specifically calls for lists.
Each major section must advance the document, not restate the title or repeat prior content.
The artifact must be usable as document source material without requiring the writer to fill major gaps.

rules: The document must begin with a single title before any body sections.
An optional short executive summary or introduction may appear after the title.
Core content must be divided into logically named sections.
Supporting lists, tables, callouts, or appendices are allowed only when they support the main narrative.
The document must end cleanly without trailing notes to self, drafting instructions, or generation metadata.
Heading levels must progress sequentially without skipping levels.
There must be exactly one H1-equivalent document title.
Primary sections must use a consistent H2-equivalent level.
Subsections must nest under the nearest parent section and use consistent lower-level headings.
Headings must be descriptive and specific, not generic labels such as 'Section 1' unless explicitly requested.
A heading must be followed by content; empty headings are invalid.
Tone must remain consistent across the full document.
The writing style must match the user's requested audience and purpose when specified.
Shifts between formal, casual, promotional, technical, or academic voice are not allowed unless the document type explicitly requires them.
Terminology must remain consistent for recurring concepts, entities, and labels.
Point of view and tense must remain stable unless a clear rhetorical reason requires a change.

completeness_checks: The document includes all major sections implied by the user's request.
No section is left as a stub, placeholder, TODO, or outline fragment.
Introductions, transitions, and conclusions are present when needed for coherence.
Examples, evidence, or supporting detail are included where the document type would reasonably require them.
The document can be converted into a generated document without major author intervention.
No critical concepts are introduced without explanation or follow-through.

general: Output must be markdown-friendly and preserve structure through plain-text formatting.
Paragraphs, headings, and lists must be clearly separable in the intermediate representation.
Whitespace must be intentional and consistent.
Inline emphasis, lists, and tables may be used when they improve readability, but formatting must not replace substantive content.
markdown_document: Use ordered or unordered lists only where semantically appropriate.
Avoid raw HTML unless explicitly required by the caller.
Code fences, tables, and blockquotes must be syntactically coherent when used.
structured_document_json: Text-bearing fields must contain final content, not references to external prose.
Hierarchy must be represented explicitly so a renderer can reconstruct the document structure deterministically.

validation_rules: output_type must be one of the supported_output_types.
Exactly one document title must be present.
At least one body section must exist.
Every section must include a non-empty heading and non-empty content.
Heading hierarchy must be valid according to heading_hierarchy_requirements.
The artifact must read as a complete document, not an outline or prompt scaffold.
The formatting must remain compatible with markdown-friendly intermediate representation constraints.
No explanatory meta-commentary may be mixed into the document body.
If JSON output is requested by the caller, the surrounding response must remain valid JSON only.
failure_conditions: Flat text dump with no section structure.
Heading levels are skipped or used inconsistently.
Sections contain placeholder text, notes to self, or TODO markers.
Tone shifts noticeably between sections without justification.
Key requested content is absent or only gestured at in outline form.
Formatting is too ambiguous to reconstruct as a document.
The output is primarily slides, spreadsheet data, code, or research notes instead of document content.
Schema fields are omitted, renamed, or supplemented in ways that break the contract.
Extra commentary appears outside the declared artifact structure.
